cases.assessmentExcerpt: Task refusal and Physical Aggression are documented. Premack Principle and Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA) are available.
paragraphs: At the dining table, the RBT placed a worksheet and pencil in front of the client and delivered a direct instruction to begin. The client manifested Task refusal by pushing the materials away and leaving the worksheet untouched. The RBT implemented Premack Principle. The RBT presented one task step before access to a preferred snack. Following this intervention, the client completed one worksheet item and returned to the page. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Compliance Training" through single-step directions; approximately 20% of discrete trials met criterion.

In the living room, the RBT placed a ball on a shelf and delayed access while presenting two alternatives. The client manifested Physical Aggression by hitting the RBT's arm with an open hand. The RBT implemented Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA). The RBT provided behavior-specific praise when the client kept hands to self. Following this intervention, the client kept both hands down and selected an alternative item. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Accept “no” as a response"; approximately 10% of discrete trials met criterion.

cases.assessmentExcerpt: Task refusal and Physical Aggression are documented.
paragraphs: At the kitchen table, the RBT presented a chore list and instructed the client to begin the first step. The client manifested Task refusal by turning away from the table and leaving the materials untouched. The RBT implemented Escape independent response delivery. The RBT re-presented smaller task steps. Following this intervention, the client returned to the table and completed one short step. The RBT implemented "Schedule of activities" by reviewing current and next tasks; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

At the counter, the RBT placed a snack out of reach and offered two alternatives. The client manifested Physical Aggression by pushing the RBT's forearm with both hands. The RBT implemented Response blocking. The RBT prevented further contact. Following this intervention, the client stopped pushing and kept both hands away from the RBT. The RBT implemented "Accepting alternatives and making choices"; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

At the laundry basket, the RBT presented a folding task and instructed the client to fold one shirt. The client manifested Task refusal by pushing clothing back into a bin. The RBT implemented Premack Principle. The RBT presented one folding step before access to a preferred toy. Following this intervention, the client folded one shirt and returned to the basket. The RBT implemented "Schedule of activities" before transitions; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

cases.assessmentExcerpt: Elopement and Property destruction are documented.

hourlyAssignments.programName: Request permission to leave the unsupervised area

paragraphs: At the dining table, the RBT placed a worksheet in front of the client and instructed the client to remain seated. The client manifested Elopement by leaving the work area and moving toward the hallway. The RBT implemented Response blocking. The RBT stopped further movement toward the hallway. Following this intervention, the client stopped advancing and returned to the chair. The RBT implemented "Request permission to leave the unsupervised area" by prompting a request before leaving the supervised area; criterion was met on approximately 30% of discrete trials.

At the desk, the RBT placed a reading page and pencil in front of the client and instructed the client to begin. The client manifested Property destruction by tearing a reading page. The RBT implemented Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA). The RBT provided praise when materials remained intact. Following this intervention, the client kept the page intact and completed one written item while remaining seated. The RBT implemented "Remaining seated, completing the task" by keeping the client seated and engaged with the assigned materials; approximately 30% of discrete trials met criterion.

cases.assessmentExcerpt: Property destruction, Self-injury behavior (SIB), and Physical Aggression are documented.

hourlyAssignments.programName: Express and accept opinion, agreement and disagreement

paragraphs: At the dining table, the RBT placed a worksheet and delivered an instruction to begin. The client manifested Property destruction by tearing a worksheet. The RBT implemented Response blocking. The RBT prevented further tearing. Following this intervention, the client stopped tearing the page and completed one short direction. The RBT implemented "Compliance Training" through short directions; criterion was met on approximately 20% of discrete trials.

Near the sofa, the RBT arranged a card game and instructed the client to take a turn. The client manifested Self-injury behavior (SIB) by striking his face with an open hand. The RBT implemented Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA). The RBT provided access to a preferred toy when hands remained away from the face. Following this intervention, the client returned to the card activity. The RBT implemented "Express and accept opinion, agreement and disagreement" through modeled statements; criterion was met on approximately 20% of discrete trials.

In the living room, the RBT delivered a cleanup direction and pointed to toys on the floor. The client manifested Physical Aggression by attempting to hit with an open hand. The RBT implemented Premack Principle. The RBT made toy access contingent on one cleanup step. Following this intervention, the client completed one cleanup step. The RBT implemented "Compliance Training" across household directions; criterion was met on approximately 20% of discrete trials.

At the desk, the RBT placed notebook materials and instructed the client to write one word. The client manifested Property destruction by tearing notebook materials. The RBT implemented Differential Reinforcement of Alternative Behavior (DRA). The RBT provided praise when materials remained intact. Following this intervention, the client kept materials intact and remained engaged with the assigned page for a short work interval. The RBT implemented "Time on Task" through short work intervals; criterion was met on approximately 20% of discrete trials.

cases.assessmentExcerpt: Task Refusal includes tossing materials aside and withdrawing from a presented task. The assessment contains broad teaching terminology that does not expand the approved profile intervention list.

profileBehaviorTargets.name: Task Refusal
profileBehaviorTargets.topography: tossing task materials aside and withdrawing from the activity

paragraphs: At the coloring table, the RBT placed a marker with a closed cap in front of the client and instructed the client to open it. The client manifested Task Refusal by tossing the marker aside and withdrawing from the activity after the cap did not open. The RBT implemented Prompting. The RBT delivered a verbal cue for requesting assistance. Following this intervention, the client used a help request with support and returned to the marker. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Request help" by reinforcing each help-seeking response; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

cases.assessmentExcerpt: The client receives home-based services. Registered behavior descriptions and approved interventions are supplied by the profile.

profileBehaviorTargets.name: Repetitive Behavior
profileBehaviorTargets.topography: continuous hand movements up and down or sideways, or tapping furniture with hands more than three times

profileBehaviorTargets.name: Tantrum
profileBehaviorTargets.topography: crying, yelling, or dropping to the floor during a presented demand

profileBehaviorTargets.name: Task Refusal
profileBehaviorTargets.topography: pushing task materials away or turning the body away from the presented task

profileBehaviorTargets.name: Physical Aggression
profileBehaviorTargets.topography: contacting another person's body with an open hand

paragraphs: At the living room table, the RBT placed picture cards representing the next activities and instructed the client to point to the first card. The client manifested Repetitive Behavior by continuous hand movements up and down. The RBT implemented Visual Supports. The RBT used picture cards to represent each activity. Following this intervention, the client stopped the continuous hand movements and pointed to one card. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Schedule of activities" by reviewing current and next pictured activities; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

At the matching table, the RBT placed matching cards in front of the client and delivered an instruction to match one card. The client manifested Tantrum by crying during the presented demand. The RBT implemented Prompting. The RBT delivered a concise verbal direction to request a break. Following this intervention, the client stopped crying and emitted a break request with support. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Request for Break" by prompting a break request before leaving the table; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

At the kitchen table, the RBT placed task materials in front of the client and instructed the client to complete one response. The client manifested Task Refusal by pushing task materials away. The RBT implemented Premack Principle. The RBT presented one brief response requirement before access to a preferred toy. Following this intervention, the client completed one response and returned to the materials. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Compliance Training" through single-step directions; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.

At the block table, the RBT placed blocks in front of the client and instructed the client to stack one block. The client manifested Physical Aggression by contacting the RBT's arm with an open hand. The RBT implemented Response blocking. The RBT prevented additional contact. Following this intervention, the client stopped contacting the RBT and selected an alternative activity card. The RBT implemented the replacement program "Accept alternatives" by presenting available options during redirection; criterion was met on approximately 10% of discrete trials.
